name: Český Krumlov Castle & Old Town Day Trip from Prague
tagline: A perfectly preserved medieval castle town that feels like a film set — 3 hours from Prague
overview: Český Krumlov is one of Central Europe's most remarkable medieval survivals — a UNESCO World Heritage town coiled around a giant castle on a bend of the Vltava river. With just 13,000 inhabitants, the entire historic centre is a living museum. The castle is the second largest in Bohemia and houses one of the finest baroque theatres in Europe.
highlights: Visit Český Krumlov Castle, second largest in Bohemia

itinerary.description: Tour the 13th-century castle complex spanning 40 buildings and 5 castle courts. Highlights include the ornate baroque theatre — one of the best preserved in Europe — and the castle gardens.
Free exploration of the UNESCO World Heritage town. Cross the Lazebnický Bridge, visit the gothic Church of St Vitus, and browse the craft shops lining the cobbled streets.

meeting_point: Prague city centre — confirmed at booking
tips: The drive from Prague is 3 hours each way — bring a book or podcast
Summer weekends are very busy; try to visit on a weekday
The castle is spectacular when viewed from across the Vltava — cross the footbridge for the best shot

faqs.question: Is castle entry included?
faqs.answer: The guided old town tour is included. Castle interior entry (approx €12) is purchased on arrival.

faqs.question: How far is Český Krumlov from Prague?
faqs.answer: Approximately 170km, a 3-hour drive through South Bohemia. The journey through the Bohemian countryside is scenic.

hero_image.alt: Český Krumlov Castle rising above the medieval town and the Vltava river bend

meta.title: Český Krumlov Castle Day Trip from Prague
meta.description: Visit Český Krumlov Castle and UNESCO old town on a guided day trip from Prague. From €35 per person. 11,500+ reviews.
